what_it_does: Records a raised decision signal that global decision d-9 has been decided with option X, and that work gated on decision://global/d-9 can resume.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-aa654b5a, address board://item-aa654b5a, type signal, source claude_code, state raised, title 'Decided: d-9 → X', author_session operator.
A links entry of kind attached_to points to decision://global/d-9.
Created and updated timestamps are identical: 2026-06-21T10:48:47.496010+00:00.
History records a single transition from null to raised by operator with note 'filed'.
Markdown body states the operator decided d-9 with chosen option X and adds a parenthetical note that the resume itself is the lane's/operator's gated step.
standouts: Decision signal with explicit chosen option X in the title and body.
attached_to link to decision://global/d-9 making the target decision explicit.
State raised with a filed history transition.
The meta-note that resuming work is itself a gated step for the lane/operator.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses board:// URI for the noticeboard item and decision://global/ URI for the attached decision.
Follows a signal/state model with a history array documenting state transitions.
Markdown body is human-readable and references the linked decision.
concerns: The chosen option X is only present in human-readable title/body and not in a structured field.
The resume actor and the specific gated work are implied but not explicitly identified.
The parenthetical note about the resume being a gated step is ambiguous about who performs it.
notes: A raised noticeboard signal announcing that global decision d-9 is resolved to option X and authorizing gated work to resume.
questions: What is the subject of decision d-9 and what were the alternative options?
Which lane or operator is responsible for performing the resume step?
What specific work items or processes are gated on decision://global/d-9?
Why is source claude_code while author_session is operator?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is a decision.decided signal and it records the decision and resume authorization.
intention_signals: To broadcast that decision d-9 is finalized with option X so that downstream lanes blocked on that decision can proceed.
novelty: Treats a noticeboard item as a durable resume token for a gated decision, with a meta-note that the act of resuming is itself a gated step.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, addressable signal linking a resolved decision to its chosen option, which a fused system can use to unblock and resume dependent work.
summary_for_embedding: This noticeboard item is a raised decision signal recording that decision d-9 was decided with option X and that work gated on decision://global/d-9 may resume.
intention_for_embedding: To broadcast the resolution of decision d-9 and authorize downstream lanes to resume work using option X.
